title: cassia javanica subsp. nodosa
intro: Cassia javanica subsp. nodosa, also known as the Indian laburnum or Indian cassia, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family and is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The medicinal properties of this plant have been utilized for centuries, with its roots and leaves exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are beneficial in treating various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Cassia javanica subsp. nodosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as a natural remedy for skin problems and wounds. The plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which are used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, indigestion, and skin conditions.
